title: Industrial Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run discrete-event simulations, optimize line balancing, and generate throughput reports end to end, but negotiating floor-level process change with operators stays human.
rationales.toolMaturity: Digital-twin and simulation-optimization tools are credible, production-deployed products at large manufacturers.
rationales.adoption: Large manufacturing and logistics operations increasingly run AI-driven simulation and scheduling tools; smaller operations lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run full simulation-optimize-report loops for line balancing and scheduling with minimal supervision.

tasksGrowing: Floor-level change management with operators and supervisors
Cross-functional process redesign for new product lines
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2112, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Simulation-driven line balancing and scheduling now run largely on their own. Industrial engineers who own operator-facing change management and ergonomics design keep the roles AI can't run unsupervised.
